message:
feat(core): enforce aggregator agent capacity with TooManyAgents error

Harden the wire protocol aggregator to reject new senders when the
tracked agent count reaches max_agents, replacing the previous
warn-and-allow behavior with a structured error. This prevents
unbounded memory growth in the agent tracking map under adversarial
or misconfigured multi-agent deployments.

wire_protocol.rs (+55 lines, -5):
  - Add WireProtocolError::TooManyAgents { max, sender } variant for
    capacity-exceeded rejections with descriptive error message
  - Aggregator::ingest() now returns TooManyAgents error and increments
    total_rejected counter when a new (unseen) sender arrives after
    max_agents is reached; existing senders are still accepted normally
  - aggregator_rejects_new_sender_over_capacity test: verifies second
    distinct sender is rejected at capacity=1, first sender accepted,
    counters correct
  - aggregator_accepts_existing_sender_at_capacity test: verifies
    same sender can continue sending at capacity=1 without rejection

distributed_streaming_e2e.rs (+62 lines):
  - Extract ingest_raw(&mut self, raw: &[u8]) helper from
    ingest_envelope(), enabling direct raw-bytes ingestion for
    malformed payload testing
  - Add replay_event_codes assertion to the gap/duplicate/reorder
    test verifying deterministic "dist.replay_detected" event code
    emission on out-of-order payloads
  - New distributed_streaming_e2e_rejects_malformed_wire_without_persisting
    test: sends malformed JSON through ingest_raw, verifies
    WireProtocolError::InvalidJson is returned, rejected counter
    increments, and no panes/segments/gaps/events are persisted to
    storage (zero side-effect guarantee for invalid input)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
